rounds_summary.observation: Crashed again at 'import shap': ModuleNotFoundError. Deferring the import did not help; 'shap' is simply absent. Evaluator not run.

ruled_out_routes: mastml.feature_generators.ElementalFeatureGenerator: package not installed in benchmark environment; import fails immediately
shap.TreeExplainer (any placement in script): 'shap' module absent from environment; ModuleNotFoundError on all attempts
sklearn.inspection.permutation_importance as SHAP substitute with matminer magpie features: executes but evaluator scores (0, 'N/A') — ranking diverges from reference SHAP ranking
synthesised_diagnosis: The environment permanently lacks 'shap', blocking rounds 1 and 3 before any computation. Round 2 avoided the import crash but substituting permutation importance for SHAP produces a different feature ranking, scoring zero because the evaluator performs exact feature-name overlap against a shap.TreeExplainer reference. No tactical import workaround can fix this: the gap is both a missing package AND a feature-ranking mismatch that only an exact SHAP implementation can close.
rationale: All three failures trace to one root: 'shap' is absent. Round 2's zero score shows permutation importance is not a drop-in evaluator-acceptable substitute — the evaluator checks exact SHAP-derived feature names, making any non-shap ranking pathway structurally incompatible.
recommended_alternative: Install 'shap' into the benchmark environment before execution (pip install shap), then use shap.TreeExplainer with the matminer ElementProperty magpie featurization pipeline from round 2 (which ran cleanly). Specifically: ep=ElementProperty.from_preset('magpie'), rf=RandomForestRegressor(n_estimators=200,random_state=42), explainer=shap.TreeExplainer(rf), mean_abs=np.abs(explainer.shap_values(X)).mean(axis=0), top20=argsort descending.
